source_refs.source_quote: 2. Adjust the air set to get a CO2 value of 11.0% – 12.5% with 0 smoke #.

source_refs.source_quote: 2. Adjust the air set to get a CO2 value of 11.0% – 12.5% with 0 smoke #.

safety_warnings.text: The heating boiler must be installed in accordance with local and state codes by a qualified installer.

source_refs.source_quote: The heating boiler must be installed in accordance with local and state codes by a qualified installer.

safety_warnings.text: Burner start-up must be performed by a qualified service person.

source_refs.source_quote: Burner start-up must be performed by a qualified service person.

safety_warnings.text: Explain the operation of the heating unit to the home owner.

source_refs.source_quote: Explain the operation of the heating unit to the home owner.

safety_warnings.text: Keep manual in a dry place near the boiler.

source_refs.source_quote: Keep manual in a dry place near the boiler.

safety_warnings.text: Consult with local authorities to insure compliance with local building, plumbing and electrical codes.

source_refs.source_quote: IMPORTANT: Consult with local authorities to insure compliance with local building, plumbing and electrical codes.

safety_warnings.text: The venting system is only for a single appliance. Venting of an additional appliance could cause serious injury or loss of life.

source_refs.source_quote: The venting system is only for a single appliance. Venting of an additional appliance could cause serious injury or loss of life.

safety_warnings.text: The venting system shall not be routed into, through, or within any other vent, such as an existing masonry or factory-built chimney.

source_refs.source_quote: The venting system shall not be routed into, through, or within any other vent, such as an existing masonry or factory-built chimney.

safety_warnings.text: Screens on intake and exhaust terminals must be kept in good working order to prevent debris from entering the venting system.

source_refs.source_quote: Screens on intake and exhaust terminals must be kept in good working order to prevent debris from entering the venting system.

safety_warnings.text: It is of vital importance to seal all flue joints and screw penetrations to prevent leakage of flue gases into the building.

source_refs.source_quote: It is of vital importance to seal all flue joints and screw penetrations to prevent leakage of flue gases into the building.

safety_warnings.text: The venting system shall not be pierced under any circumstance after initial installation.

source_refs.source_quote: The venting system shall not be pierced under any circumstance after initial installation.

safety_warnings.text: Never install a barometric damper into the exhaust piping.

source_refs.source_quote: Never install a barometric damper into the exhaust piping.

safety_warnings.text: Use only the approved materials listed on the previous page for direct venting of oil-fired Buderus G115 boilers. Use of any other materials, or systems not installed in accordance with the instructions contained in this manual will void the ITS listing.

source_refs.section_title: Installation Procedures: Exhaust Terminations and Exhaust Vent Piping.
source_refs.source_quote: NOTE: Use only the approved materials listed on the previous page for direct venting of oil-fired Buderus G115 boilers. Use of any other materials, or systems not installed in accordance with the instructions contained in this manual will void the ITS listing.

safety_warnings.text: Use strictly high temperature rated silicone (500 °F rated silicone, G.E. 106 or equivalent) and/or high temperature tape (360° F rated aluminum foil tape) for sealing at all exhaust pipe joints. Check all seams and joints of the exhaust venting for gas tightness.

source_refs.section_title: Installation Procedures: Exhaust Terminations and Exhaust Vent Piping.
source_refs.source_quote: NOTE: Use strictly high temperature rated silicone (500 °F rated silicone, G.E. 106 or equivalent) and/or high temperature tape (360° F rated aluminum foil tape) for sealing at all exhaust pipe joints. Check all seams and joints of the exhaust venting for gas tightness.

safety_warnings.text: Maintain clearances to combustibles as indicated in Table 1.

source_refs.section_title: Installation Procedures: Exhaust Terminations and Exhaust Vent Piping.
source_refs.source_quote: NOTE: Maintain clearances to combustibles as indicated in Table 1.

safety_warnings.text: Once the entire venting system is installed, make sure all joints are secure. All seams and joints of the exhaust venting must be checked for gas tightness. High temperature tape or high temperature silicone sealant can be used to ensure an air-tight venting system. It is required to have the entire system checked and serviced by a qualified technician at least once annually following initial installation.

source_refs.source_quote: Once the entire venting system is installed, make sure all joints are secure. All seams and joints of the exhaust venting must be checked for gas tightness. High temperature tape or high temperature silicone sealant can be used to ensure an air-tight venting system. It is required to have the entire system checked and serviced by a qualified technician at least once annually following initial installation.

safety_warnings.text: ADVISE OWNER to keep intake and exhaust terminations free of debris and snow.

source_refs.source_quote: ADVISE OWNER to keep intake and exhaust terminations free of debris and snow.

safety_warnings.text: Buderus Hydronic Systems, Inc. will not assume any responsibility for the possible effects of an obstructed air intake or exhaust termination.

source_refs.source_quote: Warning: Buderus Hydronic Systems, Inc. will not assume any responsibility for the possible effects of an obstructed air intake or exhaust termination.

safety_warnings.text: MAKE SURE THE BURNER OBSERVATION PORT LOCATED ON THE BURNER DOOR IS SEALED AIR-TIGHT BY PROPERLY TIGHTENING THE PORT SCREW. USE EXTRA SILICONE SEALANT IF NECESSARY. TIGHTEN BURNER DOOR BOLTS EVENLY AND ENSURE AN AIR-TIGHT SEAL.

source_refs.source_quote: NOTE: MAKE SURE THE BURNER OBSERVATION PORT LOCATED ON THE BURNER DOOR IS SEALED AIR-TIGHT BY PROPERLY TIGHTENING THE PORT SCREW. USE EXTRA SILICONE SEALANT IF NECESSARY. TIGHTEN BURNER DOOR BOLTS EVENLY AND ENSURE AN AIR-TIGHT SEAL.

safety_warnings.text: SURFACE DISCOLORATION ON THE OUTSIDE OF THE BUILDING MAY OCCUR IF THE BURNER IS NOT PROPERLY ADJUSTED. BUDERUS HYDRONIC SYSTEMS, INC. WILL NOT ACCEPT ANY RESPONSIBILITY FOR SUCH DISCOLORATION.

source_refs.source_quote: NOTE: SURFACE DISCOLORATION ON THE OUTSIDE OF THE BUILDING MAY OCCUR IF THE BURNER IS NOT PROPERLY ADJUSTED. BUDERUS HYDRONIC SYSTEMS, INC. WILL NOT ACCEPT ANY RESPONSIBILITY FOR SUCH DISCOLORATION.

safety_warnings.text: Buderus Hydronic Systems, Inc. strongly recommends the use of a low micron oil filter such as the Garber oil filter, placed in the fuel line near the burner. This filter is in addition to a regular oil filter placed at the outlet of the oil tank.

source_refs.source_quote: NOTE: Buderus Hydronic Systems, Inc. strongly recommends the use of a low micron oil filter such as the Garber oil filter, placed in the fuel line near the burner. This filter is in addition to a regular oil filter placed at the outlet of the oil tank.

safety_warnings.text: Always use instruments to check combustion. DO NOT ADJUST BURNER OPERATION BY EYE!

source_refs.source_quote: Always use instruments to check combustion. DO NOT ADJUST BURNER OPERATION BY EYE!

safety_warnings.text: Stop burner operation at once if smoke emerges from the exhaust terminal. Check and make necessary burner adjustments to obtain smoke-free flue products.

source_refs.source_quote: NOTE: Stop burner operation at once if smoke emerges from the exhaust terminal. Check and make necessary burner adjustments to obtain smoke-free flue products.

safety_warnings.text: MAKE SURE the burner observation port located on the burner door is sealed air-tight by properly tightening the port screw. Use extra sealant if necessary.

source_refs.source_quote: NOTE: MAKE SURE the burner observation port located on the burner door is sealed air-tight by properly tightening the port screw. Use extra sealant if necessary.

safety_warnings.text: Never tamper with boiler or burner controls and settings.

source_refs.source_quote: 1. Never tamper with boiler or burner controls and settings.

safety_warnings.text: Never place combustible materials within 5 ft of the boiler.

source_refs.source_quote: 2. Never place combustible materials within 5 ft of the boiler.

safety_warnings.text: Only use fuel oil #2 or kerosene for your oil boiler. Never use gasoline, waste oil or other combustible materials in your boiler.

source_refs.source_quote: 3. Only use fuel oil #2 or kerosene for your oil boiler. Never use gasoline, waste oil or other combustible materials in your boiler.

safety_warnings.text: Never ignite any material in your boiler. The unit is designed for hands-off operation.

source_refs.source_quote: 4. Never ignite any material in your boiler. The unit is designed for hands-off operation.

safety_warnings.text: Do not plant vegetation directly under vent termination.

source_refs.source_quote: 5. Do not plant vegetation directly under vent termination.

safety_warnings.text: Regularly check the air intake and exhaust terminals for any blockage or debris that could hinder the flow of combustion air to the burner and flue gases from the boiler. Any obstructions could jeopardize clean combustion and could result in poor combustion, smoky and fuel smelling flue gases.

source_refs.source_quote: Regularly check the air intake and exhaust terminals for any blockage or debris that could hinder the flow of combustion air to the burner and flue gases from the boiler. Any obstructions could jeopardize clean combustion and could result in poor combustion, smoky and fuel smelling flue gases.

maintenance_tasks.task_name: Inspect and service boiler and vent system
maintenance_tasks.description: To maintain efficient operation of the system.

source_refs.source_quote: The boiler and vent system should be inspected and serviced once annually by a qualified service person to maintain efficient operation of the system.

maintenance_tasks.task_name: Verify proper operation of boiler controls and safety devices

source_refs.source_quote: Proper operation of boiler controls and safety devices must be verified during the annual service.

maintenance_tasks.task_name: Disconnect electrical power, fuel supply, and air intake duct
maintenance_tasks.description: Disconnect electrical power to the system and the fuel supply and air intake duct to the burner. Remove front panel jacket from the boiler. Remove burner door bolts and swing open burner door.

source_refs.source_quote: 1. Disconnect the electrical power to the system and the fuel supply and air intake duct to the burner. Remove front panel jacket from the boiler. Remove burner door bolts and swing open burner door.

maintenance_tasks.task_name: Clean combustion chamber and heat exchanger
maintenance_tasks.description: Brush the combustion chamber and heat exchanger surface with a flexible bristle brush and remove deposits with a vacuum cleaner.

source_refs.source_quote: 2. Brush the combustion chamber and heat exchanger surface with a flexible bristle brush and remove deposits with a vacuum cleaner.

maintenance_tasks.task_name: Clean flue pipe and air intake pipe
maintenance_tasks.description: Tap gently against flue pipe to loosen possible build-up and slide vacuum hose through the upper portion of the heat exchanger into the flue pipe to remove deposits. Make sure that no obstructions are present in the flue pipe and air intake pipe.

source_refs.source_quote: 3. Tap gently against flue pipe to loosen possible build-up and slide vacuum hose through the upper portion of the heat exchanger into the flue pipe to remove deposits. Make sure that no obstructions are present in the flue pipe and air intake pipe.

maintenance_tasks.task_name: Ensure boiler and venting system is gas tight
maintenance_tasks.description: Close burner door, tighten burner door bolts evenly and ensure that entire boiler and venting system is gas tight.

source_refs.source_quote: 4. Close burner door, tighten burner door bolts evenly and ensure that entire boiler and venting system is gas tight.

maintenance_tasks.description: Service oil burner in accordance with oil burner manufacturer's instructions.

source_refs.source_quote: 5. Service oil burner in accordance with oil burner manufacturer's instructions.

maintenance_tasks.description: Test the Garber oil filter cartridge with a vacuum gauge under full purge. Compare reading to original vacuum reading of the initial installation and replace if necessary.

source_refs.source_quote: 6. Test the Garber oil filter cartridge with a vacuum gauge under full purge. Compare reading to original vacuum reading of the initial installation and replace if necessary.

maintenance_tasks.task_name: Replace oil filter at oil tank discharge

source_refs.source_quote: 7. Replace the oil filter at the oil tank discharge.

maintenance_tasks.description: Reinstall all shields and covers, reconnect fuel line and bleed air from oil delivery supply and connect electrical power to the boiler.

source_refs.source_quote: 8. Reinstall all shields and covers, reconnect fuel line and bleed air from oil delivery supply and connect electrical power to the boiler.

maintenance_tasks.description: Perform complete combustion test with instruments to ensure proper burner operation. Make necessary adjustments.

source_refs.source_quote: 9. Perform complete combustion test with instruments to ensure proper burner operation. Make necessary adjustments.

maintenance_tasks.task_name: Seal test ports and verify exhaust venting system air-tightness
maintenance_tasks.description: Make sure all test ports are properly sealed after completion of combustion test. Verify that the exhaust venting system is air-tight. Use silicone sealant if necessary.

source_refs.source_quote: 10. Make sure all test ports are properly sealed after completion of combustion test. Verify that the exhaust venting system is air-tight. Use silicone sealant if necessary.

maintenance_tasks.description: Temporarily disconnect power to the heating circulator(s), turn room thermostat to a high setting to fire the burner and verify proper operation of the high limit control. After verification, turn room thermostat to original setting and restore power to the circulator(s).

source_refs.section_title: Testing of boiler controls and safety devices.
source_refs.source_quote: Temporarily disconnect power to the heating circulator(s), turn room thermostat to a high setting to fire the burner and verify proper operation of the high limit control. After verification, turn room thermostat to original setting and restore power to the circulator(s).

maintenance_tasks.task_name: Verify cover plates, guards, and vent pipe connections
maintenance_tasks.description: Verify that all cover plates, guards and vent pipe connections are properly in place. Make sure that the exhaust venting is gas tight. Use silicone sealant if necessary.

source_refs.section_title: Testing of boiler controls and safety devices.
source_refs.source_quote: NOTE: Upon completion of the annual service, verify that all cover plates, guards and vent pipe connections are properly in place. Make sure that the exhaust venting is gas tight. Use silicone sealant if necessary.

maintenance_tasks.description: To maintain highly efficient operation and avoid unnecessary expenses.

source_refs.source_quote: Your boiler/burner unit should be serviced at least once annually by a qualified service person to maintain highly efficient operation and avoid unnecessary expenses.

maintenance_tasks.description: Especially during the summer to prevent moisture condensing and build-up on the inside surface of the fuel tank.

source_refs.source_quote: Keep your fuel tank full, especially during the summer to prevent moisture condensing and build-up on the inside surface of the fuel tank.

maintenance_tasks.task_name: Check air intake and exhaust terminals for blockage
maintenance_tasks.description: Regularly check the air intake and exhaust terminals for any blockage or debris that could hinder the flow of combustion air to the burner and flue gases from the boiler.

source_refs.source_quote: Regularly check the air intake and exhaust terminals for any blockage or debris that could hinder the flow of combustion air to the burner and flue gases from the boiler.

extraction_notes: No explicit fault, diagnostic, or status code tables were found in the manual. Homeowner troubleshooting steps were extracted as general information.
No fault codes, diagnostic codes, or status codes were extracted. Manual should be reviewed or reprocessed.
